### Post by adam212 on 2007-12-01
Ok gentlemen, i have tried everything, the updates, searched the forums and tried every combination/permutation to get my video card to display 1440x900 on my hp wide 19' but it has been useless so far. I couldnt fix it last time so lets try again. Please help me. Here is the xorg.conf.

> # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"

	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"

	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"

	Identifier	"stylus"

	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"

	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82G965 Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"HP w19b/w19e"
	HorizSync 	30.0 - 82.0
	VertRefresh 	50.0 - 85.0	
	Option		"DPMS"
	Modeline 	"1440x900_70.00" 126.98 1440 1536 1688 1936 900 901 904 937 -HSync +Vsync

EndSection

Section "Screen"

EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"

	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

### Post by david_2001 on 2007-12-01
First thing to do is comment out or delete the "DisplaySize 1440 900" line in the Monitor section.  From the xorg.conf manual:
```

       DisplaySize  width height
              This optional entry gives the width and height, **in  millimetres**,
              of  the  picture  area of the monitor.  If given this is used to
              calculate the horizontal and vertical pitch (DPI) of the screen.
```
Even if correctly set, it shouldn't be necessary to include DisplaySize with recent versions of x.org because it gets calculated automatically.

---

### Post by david_2001 on 2007-12-02
